threads.content:
A $50,000 animated video project now costs under $200 to produce.

I am not exaggerating. I have seen three companies replace their entire motion graphics pipeline with AI-powered HTML-to-video tools in the last 60 days.

Are you selling production or strategy?

Brands are pouring money into blog content that nobody reads while video outperforms text by 3-5x on every engagement metric.

I analyzed 200 B2B content strategies last quarter. The ones growing fastest had one thing in common: video as the primary format, text as the supporting format.

The old rule was "write a blog post, maybe make a video if budget allows." The new rule is "make a video first, extract text from it."

What percentage of your content is video today?

instagram.caption:
HTML + CSS = MP4. No camera, no editor, no rendering pipeline. AI agents just got a video factory. How would you use code-to-video? Follow for more on AI media tools.

#ai #artificialintelligence #tech #developer #startup #coding #automation #futureofwork

Save this for later. Follow @founderswing for daily AI breakdowns.

What do you think? Drop your take below.

tweets:
A $50,000 animated video project now costs under $200 to produce.

A thread 🧵👇

The agency model was built on two things: creative talent and production labor. AI is eating the production labor part at a pace that would have seemed absurd 12 months ago. What used to require a designer, an animator, a compositor, and a render farm now requires a prompt and a template.

The agencies that will survive are not the ones with the best software. They are the ones with the best strategic thinking. Clients do not need you to make a video anymore. They need you to decide which video to make, for whom, and why.

That is a consulting business, not a production business.
The transition will be brutal for agencies that cannot reposition. But for solo strategists who understand AI production tools, this is the widest-open market I have seen in a decade.
Are you selling production or strategy?
